# @munesoft/logx

> **A tiny, zero-config logger for Node.js and browser.**  
> Structured, beautiful, and fast — without the complexity.

[![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/@munesoft/logx)](https://npmjs.com/package/@munesoft/logx)
[![license](https://img.shields.io/npm/l/@munesoft/logx)](./LICENSE)
[![bundle size](https://img.shields.io/bundlephobia/minzip/@munesoft/logx)](https://bundlephobia.com/package/@munesoft/logx)

---

## 🚀 Why logx?

Most logging libraries are too complex, too heavy, or require pages of setup.

`logx` is:

- **Simple** — feels exactly like `console.log`
- **Fast** — 1–3M ops/s, zero heavy dependencies
- **Structured** — every log is internally a typed object
- **Beautiful** — colored output, clean stack traces, readable key=val pairs
- **Universal** — Node.js + browser, ESM + CJS

---

## ⚡ Quick Start

```bash
npm install @munesoft/logx
```

```js
import logx from '@munesoft/logx';

logx('User created', { id: 123 });
// ✔ User created  id=123
```

**Zero setup. Just import and go.**

---

## 🔥 Features

| Feature | Description |
|---|---|
| Structured logging | Every log carries typed metadata |
| Log levels | `info`, `warn`, `error`, `debug` with smart defaults |
| Colored output | ANSI colors in Node, `%c` styling in browser |
| JSON mode | Emit newline-delimited JSON for log aggregators |
| Child loggers | Bind persistent context to a logger instance |
| Transports | Route logs to any destination |
| Filters | Programmatically suppress log levels |
| Grouping | `console.group`-style nesting |
| Error handling | Auto-detect `Error` objects, format stack traces |
| Circular refs | Safe serialization — never throws |
| `sprintf` formatting | `logx('User %s created', name)` |
| Zero config | Works out of the box |
| Tree-shakeable | ESM, pure module |
| TypeScript | Full `.d.ts` included |

---

## 📊 Examples

### Basic

```js
logx('Hello world');
// ✔ Hello world
```

### With Data

```js
logx('User created', { id: 1, role: 'admin' });
// ✔ User created  id=1 role=admin
```

### Log Levels

```js
logx.info('Server started', { port: 3000 });
// ✔ Server started  port=3000

logx.warn('Rate limit approaching', { current: 90, limit: 100 });
// ⚠ Rate limit approaching  current=90 limit=100

logx.error('Database error', new Error('ECONNREFUSED'));
// ✖ Database error
//   Error: ECONNREFUSED
//     at ...

logx.debug('Cache miss', { key: 'users:123' });
// ◆ Cache miss  key=users:123
```

### JSON Output

Perfect for production log aggregation (Datadog, Loki, CloudWatch, etc.):

```js
logx.json('User created', { id: 1 });
// {"level":"info","message":"User created","timestamp":1714230000,"id":1}
```

Enable JSON mode globally:

```js
logx.config({ json: true });
```

### Child Loggers

Bind context to a logger — every message from it automatically includes that context:

```js
const apiLog = logx.child({ service: 'api', version: '2' });

apiLog('Request received', { path: '/users', method: 'GET' });
// ✔ Request received  service=api version=2 path=/users method=GET

apiLog.warn('Slow response', { ms: 2300 });
// ⚠ Slow response  service=api version=2 ms=2300
```

Nest child loggers:

```js
const reqLog = apiLog.child({ requestId: 'abc-123' });
reqLog('Processing');
// ✔ Processing  service=api version=2 requestId=abc-123
```

### Error Handling

Pass `Error` objects directly — logx detects and formats them automatically:

```js
try {
  await db.query(sql);
} catch (err) {
  logx.error('Query failed', err, { sql });
}
// ✖ Query failed  sql=SELECT...
//   Error: connection timeout
//     at Database.query (db.js:45:12)
//     at ...
```

### Sprintf-Style Formatting

```js
logx('User %s logged in from %s', username, ip);
logx('Processed %d items in %dms', count, duration);
```

### Transports

Route logs to external systems:

```js
logx.use((entry) => {
  fetch('/api/logs', {
    method: 'POST',
    body: JSON.stringify(entry),
  });
});
```

Return `false` to suppress default console output:

```js
logx.use((entry) => {
  myLogger.write(entry);
  return false; // don't also print to console
});
```

### Filters

```js
// Suppress debug logs in staging
logx.filter((level) => level !== 'debug');

// Only log errors and warnings  
logx.filter((level) => ['error', 'warn'].includes(level));
```

### Grouping

```js
logx.group('Auth Flow', () => {
  logx('Checking credentials');
  logx('Validating token', { userId: 7 });
  logx('Session created');
});
```

### Configuration

```js
logx.config({
  level:     'debug',   // 'silent' | 'error' | 'warn' | 'info' | 'debug'
  pretty:    true,      // formatted output (vs raw)
  json:      false,     // force JSON mode
  timestamp: true,      // include timestamp in every log
  silent:    false,     // suppress all output
});
```

One-call silence:

```js
logx({ silent: true });
```

### Async Context

Attach context that will appear on all subsequent logs (useful in request middleware):

```js
app.use((req, res, next) => {
  logx.context({ requestId: req.headers['x-request-id'] });
  next();
});
```

---

## 🆚 Comparison

| Feature | `console.log` | `pino` | `winston` | **logx** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Zero config | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Structured | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Colored output | ❌ | requires plugin | requires plugin | ✅ |
| JSON mode | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Child loggers | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Browser support | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| TypeScript | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Bundle size | 0 kb | ~50 kb | ~100 kb | < 5 kb |
| Setup required | none | low | high | **none** |

---

## 📦 Install & Import

```bash
npm install @munesoft/logx
# or
yarn add @munesoft/logx
```

**ESM:**
```js
import logx from '@munesoft/logx';
```

**CJS:**
```js
const logx = require('@munesoft/logx');
```

**Browser (CDN):**
```html
<script type="module">
  import logx from 'https://cdn.skypack.dev/@munesoft/logx';
  logx('Hello from browser!');
</script>
```

---

## ⚙️ Environment Awareness

logx automatically adapts:

| Environment | Default Level | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `NODE_ENV=development` | `debug` | All levels visible |
| `NODE_ENV=production` | `info` | Debug suppressed |
| `NO_COLOR=1` | any | ANSI colors disabled |
| Browser (localhost) | `debug` | Dev mode detected |
| Browser (production) | `info` | Minimal output |

---

## 🏎️ Performance

Benchmarked on Node.js 22, Apple M-series:

```
logx("Hello world")               1,006,100 ops/s   0.99 µs/op
logx("Msg", { id: 1 })            1,178,384 ops/s   0.85 µs/op
logx.info("Info", { x: 1 })       2,315,195 ops/s   0.43 µs/op
logx.json("Event", { ok: true })  1,507,092 ops/s   0.66 µs/op
child("Msg", { id: 1 })           2,290,502 ops/s   0.44 µs/op
logx with circular ref            1,582,889 ops/s   0.63 µs/op
```

---

## 🏗️ Architecture

```
src/
├── core/       # Main logging engine, config, child loggers, transports
├── format/     # Pretty (ANSI) + JSON + browser (%c) formatters
├── color/      # Zero-dependency ANSI color utilities
├── levels/     # Level constants and comparison logic  
├── utils/      # sprintf, safe serialization, env detection, timestamps
└── browser/    # Browser-specific console adapter
```

No runtime dependencies. Zero external packages.

---

```bash
npm test       # 27 unit tests
npm run bench  # performance benchmarks
```
